page: Microsoft Azure L8s_v4 is a storage-optimized server instance from the Lsv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 2 threads per core. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB per core, and includes 1920 GB of local storage. The instance does not include a GPU or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-threaded performance, with gzip compression reaching 31.37 GB/sec and a Geekbench single-core score of 2168.0, both placing in the top 25% of tested servers. Text processing performance is also strong at 2704.0. Multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally fall into the average range, with a Passmark CPU score of 14267.95 and a multi-core Geekbench score of 8333.0. Large block read-only memory bandwidth is measured at 65.68 GB/sec, while write bandwidth is 45.55 GB/sec. For database operations, the instance achieves an average Passmark database score of 5261.65 and handles 11.48 million operations per second in Redis SET tests.

The L8s_v4 offers high local storage density and a generous memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads requiring substantial local storage without the overhead of larger compute configurations. However, tradeoffs exist in compute-heavy and machine learning tasks. While text generation for small LLM models is strong at 270.03 tokens/sec, text generation for medium LLM models is weak at 8.36 tokens/sec, and large model text generation is unsupported due to the lack of a GPU. This server is best fit for database operations, local storage-heavy applications, static web serving, and general-purpose workloads requiring high-speed local storage.
description: Microsoft Azure L8s_v4 is an x86_64 storage-optimized server instance from the Lsv4 family, utilizing a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor. Operating at 4.2 GHz, it provides 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 1920 GB of local storage, with no GPU. Benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, with gzip compression at 31.37 GB/sec and a Geekbench single-core score of 2168.0. Multi-core performance is average, yielding a Passmark CPU score of 14267.95 and a Redis SET throughput of 11.48 million operations per second. The instance offers high storage density relative to its compute resources, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-intensive tasks. However, it exhibits weak performance in medium-sized LLM text generation. It is best suited for database operations, static web serving, and storage-heavy workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L8s_v4 is an x86_64 storage-optimized server featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1920 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core performance and average database throughput.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L8s_v4 is an x86_64 storage-optimized server with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1920 GB local storage for database and storage-heavy workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 storage-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon compute, 64 GB RAM, and 1920 GB of bundled local storage.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 8 vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
64.0 GB of system memory paired with 1920 GB of local storage
Strong single-core performance in gzip compression and text processing benchmarks
Average multi-core performance with 11.48 million Redis SET operations per second
Weak text generation performance for medium-sized LLM models due to lack of GPU
Best fit for database operations, static web serving, and storage-heavy workloads
